release_note:
- Etherpad v3.1.0 adds Tier 4 autonomous self-updates with maintenance windows, preflight checks, and email notifications for failures and rollbacks.
- It also introduces real SMTP delivery for updater mail and includes API, export, and HTTP security hardening plus localisation updates.
description:
Etherpad is a real-time collaborative document editor where multiple people can edit the same pad simultaneously, with every change synchronised instantly and colour-coded per author.
Self-hosted, open source, and fully under your control, it is perfect for meeting notes, brainstorming, shared writing, and lightweight knowledge capture.
It ships with a built-in admin panel, a plugin ecosystem of 250+ add-ons, native DOCX/PDF/Markdown import & export, and multi-architecture container support (amd64 + arm64).

**Main Features:**
- Real-time collaborative editing with instant synchronisation
- Per-author colour coding for clear writing attribution
- Built-in admin panel and a plugin ecosystem with 250+ add-ons
- Native DOCX, PDF, and Markdown import & export
- Open source, self-hosted deployment with amd64 and arm64 support

**Learn More:**
- [Etherpad Official Website](https://etherpad.org/)
- [Etherpad GitHub](https://github.com/ether/etherpad)
- [Etherpad Documentation](https://docs.etherpad.org/)

tips.before_install: Default admin password is "admin". Change ADMIN_PASSWORD before exposing Etherpad to the network. Pads are stored on-disk in /DATA/AppData/etherpad/var using the embedded dirty database; this is ideal for personal and small-team use. For heavy multi-user production workloads, switch DB_TYPE to postgres or mysql and point DB_HOST, DB_NAME, DB_USER, and DB_PASS at an external database.
